# PlayOrbs — Full Documentation

> Decentralized Battle Royale powered by Solana & ICP

This file contains the complete PlayOrbs documentation for LLM consumption.

- Website: https://playorbs.com
- Documentation: https://docs.playorbs.com
- Solana: https://solana.com
- Internet Computer: https://internetcomputer.org

---

# What is PlayOrbs?

**PlayOrbs** is a decentralized battle royale gaming platform powered by two leading blockchains: Solana for high-performance on-chain execution and the Internet Computer (ICP) for provably fair randomness. Players compete in tiered rounds, paying entry fees in SOL for a chance to win prize pools and earn PORB tokens.

## Value Proposition

- **Fair Launch Economics**: No pre-mine, no team allocation - tokens are earned through play
- **Real Liquidity Backing**: Every PORB token is backed by accumulated game fees
- **Dual-Chain Security**: Solana handles settlements while ICP provides cryptographic randomness

## Key Differentiators

| Feature | PlayOrbs | Traditional P2E |
|---------|-----------|-----------------|
| Token Distribution | Earned through gameplay | Pre-allocated to team/investors |
| Liquidity Source | Accumulated game fees | External capital injection |
| Token Utility | Prize pools, emissions, governance | Often speculative only |
| Settlement | Verifiable on-chain | Often centralized servers |

## Powered By

### Solana — Execution Layer
Built with Anchor 0.32.1, the on-chain program manages:
- Round creation and settlement
- Prize pool distribution
- Token emissions
- Player statistics

### Internet Computer (ICP) — Randomness Layer
ICP canisters provide threshold cryptography for:
- Unpredictable seed generation
- Verifiable fairness via Merkle proofs
- Tamper-proof settlement that no one can manipulate

### Deterministic Physics
The 120Hz game engine guarantees:
- Byte-perfect replay across platforms
- Trustless verification
- Fair competition

---

# One Pager

**The provably fair battle royale powered by Solana & ICP.**

## The Problem

Web3 gaming is broken. Players face:
- **Rigged outcomes** — Centralized RNG that operators can manipulate
- **Ponzi tokenomics** — Inflationary tokens backed by nothing but hope
- **Rug-prone liquidity** — Team-controlled pools that vanish overnight
- **Pay-to-win mechanics** — Deep pockets beat skill every time

## The Solution

PlayOrbs reimagines competitive gaming with trustless infrastructure.

PAY SOL → COMPETE → WIN SOL
- No tokens required to play
- Entry fees fund prize pools
- Physics-based battle royale, deterministic and verifiable

## How It Works

| Step | Action | Result |
|:----:|--------|--------|
| 1 | Pay SOL entry fee | Join the arena |
| 2 | Battle royale begins | Physics engine determines winner |
| 3 | ICP provides seed | Cryptographically verified outcome |
| 4 | Winners paid instantly | SOL + Points + PORB tokens |

Zero trust required. Every outcome is verifiable on-chain.

## The PORB Token

100% fair launch. Zero pre-mine. Zero VC allocation.

- Total Supply: 100,000,000 PORB
- Team Allocation: 0%
- VC Allocation: 0%
- Treasury: 0%
- Gameplay: 100%

### Liquidity-Backed Value

Every PORB is backed by real game fees:
- 20% of all entry fees flow to protocol
- 50% of protocol fees fund LP vault
- Continuous buybacks create demand
- Locked liquidity — permanently. No rug possible.

## Why PlayOrbs Wins

| Traditional Gaming | PlayOrbs |
|--------------------|----------|
| Trust the operator | Trust the math |
| Inflationary tokens | Hard cap at 100M |
| Team controls liquidity | Liquidity locked forever |
| Opaque RNG | ICP threshold cryptography |
| Play to grind | Play to compete |

## The Numbers

- Prize Split: 80% to players (70% bounty/kills, 30% survival)
- Fee Split: 80% prizes, 20% protocol (LP + Dev)
- Emissions: 15% decay per epoch, 100+ year longevity

## Dual-Chain Architecture

ICP (internetcomputer.org): Threshold Randomness → Merkle Trees → ECDSA Signatures
- WHY ICP: Decentralized RNG that no one can predict or rig

Solana (solana.com): Verify Proofs → Execute Rounds → Reward Winners
- WHY SOLANA: Fast, cheap transactions with locked liquidity

---

# How It Works

The game operates as a simple loop: enter, compete, and earn rewards based on your performance.

## The Game Loop

### 1. Entry
Players pay SOL to join a round in their chosen tier. Each tier has different:
- Entry fees (higher tiers = bigger rewards)
- Player limits
- Countdown durations
- Points multipliers

### 2. Competition
Battle royale gameplay happens off-chain in a deterministic physics engine:
- All players spawn in the arena
- Orbs collide, break tethers, and eliminate opponents
- Last orb standing wins

### 3. Settlement
After the round ends:
- A cryptographic seed from the ICP canister determines final outcomes
- Winners are verified on-chain
- Prizes are distributed automatically

### 4. Rewards
Participants earn multiple reward types:
- **SOL Prizes**: Winners receive portions of the prize pool
- **Points**: All players earn points based on performance
- **PORB Tokens**: First 3 joiners may receive token emissions

## Fee Distribution

Entry Fee (100%)
- 80% → Prize Pool (winners)
- 20% → Protocol
  - 50% → LP Vault (liquidity)
  - 50% → Dev Wallet
    - 90% → Development
    - 10% → Referral Rewards

## Round States

| State | Description | What Happens |
|-------|-------------|--------------|
| **Open** | Accepting players | Players join, prize pool grows |
| **Countdown** | Timer running | Minimum players reached, countdown starts |
| **Settled** | Round complete | Winners determined, prizes distributed |

## Settlement Verification

1. **ICP Canister** generates a cryptographic seed
2. **Merkle Proof** binds the seed to the specific round
3. **ECDSA Signature** proves authenticity
4. **On-chain Verification** confirms everything before settlement

This means no one—not even the operators—can predict or manipulate outcomes.

---

# Key Features

## Tiered Competition

Multiple entry tiers cater to different risk appetites. Higher tiers offer:
- Larger prize pools
- Higher points multipliers
- More competitive opponents

## Seasonal Leaderboards

- **60,000 rounds per season** (~3 months)
- Points accumulate throughout the season
- Top performers share the season pool
- Rankings reset each season for fresh competition

## Token Emissions

| Recipient | Amount per Emission |
|-----------|---------------------|
| 1st Joiner | 100 PORB |
| 2nd Joiner | 60 PORB |
| 3rd Joiner | 40 PORB |
| Season Pool | 50 PORB |

Emission probability:
- Increases linearly since last emission
- Guaranteed by round 20
- Average: ~1 emission per 10 rounds

## Early Exit (EE)

An optional early exit mechanism:
- Set a target earnings when joining
- Automatically exit if target is hit
- Reduces risk but may limit maximum gains

## Referral Program

Invite friends and earn:
- 10% of referred players' entry fees (SOL)
- 20 points per referred player join
- Milestone bonuses at 50 and 250 referrals

## Achievement System

### Seasonal Achievements (reset each season)
- First Blood - Play 1 round
- Getting Warm - Play 5 rounds
- Committed - Play 25 rounds
- Champion - Win 1 round
- Killing Spree - 3+ kills in one round
- Hot Streak - 2 consecutive wins

### Lifetime Achievements (one-time)
- Nickname Pioneer - First to set nickname
- Referrer Pro - Refer 50 players
- Referrer Legend - Refer 250 players

## Fair Launch Economics

- **No pre-mine**: Zero tokens allocated to team
- **No VC rounds**: No investor allocations
- **Earned only**: 100% of tokens come from gameplay
- **Liquidity-backed**: Every PORB backed by real game fees

## Provably Fair Randomness

- Seeds from ICP's threshold cryptography
- Merkle proofs bind seeds to rounds
- Anyone can verify fairness independently

---

# Getting Started

## Prerequisites

1. **Solana Wallet**: Phantom, Solflare, or any Solana-compatible wallet
2. **SOL Balance**: Enough to cover entry fees + transaction costs (~0.001 SOL per tx)
3. **Game Client**: Access via the official PlayOrbs interface

## Connecting Your Wallet

1. Navigate to the PlayOrbs platform
2. Click "Connect Wallet"
3. Select your wallet provider
4. Approve the connection

## Your First Round

### Step 1: Choose a Tier
Select a tier based on your risk tolerance and SOL balance.

### Step 2: Join a Round
1. Click "Join" on your chosen tier
2. Review the entry fee and round details
3. Confirm the transaction in your wallet

### Step 3: Wait for Countdown
Once minimum players join, the countdown timer starts.

### Step 4: Compete
- Your orb spawns in the arena
- Collide with opponents to eliminate them
- Break tethers to score kills
- Survive as long as possible

### Step 5: Collect Rewards
- Winners receive SOL prizes automatically
- All players earn points based on performance
- First 3 joiners may receive PORB emissions

## Tips for New Players

1. Start with Tier 0: Lower entry while learning
2. Watch the countdown: More players = bigger prize pool
3. Check emission status: Being first to join has advantages
4. Track your points: They accumulate for season rewards
5. Set a referral link: Earn from players you invite

---

# Round Mechanics

Every PlayOrbs round follows a predictable lifecycle with three distinct states.

## State Details

### Open State
The round begins when the first player joins:
- Accept new players, accumulate prize pool
- 80% to vault, 20% to protocol

### Countdown State
Triggered when minimum player threshold is met:
- Countdown timer visible to all
- New players can still join
- Ends when timer expires OR max players reached

### Settled State
The terminal state after competition:
- Cryptographic seed is set
- Game outcome is determined
- SOL prizes distributed to winners
- Points awarded to all participants
- PORB emissions processed (if triggered)

## Round Data Structure

```
RoundMeta {
    id: u64,
    season_id: u16,
    tier_id: u8,
    status: u8,               // 0=Open, 1=Countdown, 2=Settled
    countdown_start_ts: i64,
    entry_lamports: u64,
    min_players: u16,
    max_players: u16,
    countdown_secs: u32,
    seed_hex: [u8; 32],
    joined_count: u16,
    created_ts: i64,
}
```

## Settlement Eligibility

```
settlement_allowed = (status == COUNTDOWN) AND (
    (now >= countdown_start_ts + countdown_secs)  // Timeout
    OR (joined_count == max_players)              // Full
)
```

---

# Tier System

Tiers define different competition levels with varying entry fees, player limits, and rewards.

## Tier Configuration

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| tier_id | Unique identifier (0-15) |
| entry_lamports | Entry fee in lamports |
| min_players | Minimum to start countdown |
| max_players | Maximum capacity |
| countdown_secs | Countdown duration |
| take_profit | Optional TP fee |
| points_multiplier | Scoring multiplier |

## Points Multiplier Effect

| Tier | Base Points | Multiplier | Final Points |
|------|-------------|------------|--------------|
| 0 | 850 | 1.0x | 850 |
| 1 | 850 | 1.5x | 1,275 |
| 2 | 850 | 2.0x | 1,700 |
| 3 | 850 | 3.0x | 2,550 |

---

# Seasons

PlayOrbs operates in seasons, providing regular reset points and fresh competition.

## Season Parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Rounds per season | 60,000 |
| Approximate duration | ~3 months |
| Pool source | 50 PORB per emission |

## Season 0 (Genesis Phase)

| Feature | Season 0 | Season 1+ |
|---------|----------|-----------|
| PORB Emissions | Disabled | Enabled |
| Points | Accumulate for conversion | Earn direct rewards |
| LP Fees | Accumulate toward genesis | Normal operation |
| Season Pool | None | 50 PORB per emission |

After 25 SOL accumulates in the LP vault:
1. Genesis triggers
2. Points convert to PORB (50 points = 1 PORB)
3. Season 1 begins with emissions enabled

## Claiming Rewards

After a season ends:
1. Snapshot Created: Final rankings frozen
2. Claims Open: Players can claim their share
3. Calculation: Your Share = (Your Points / Total Points) × Pool

---

# Matrix Game

The Matrix is a memory-based mini-game played before each round. Your performance determines how many Skill Points (SP) you earn to allocate across your orb's abilities.

## Game Phases

### Phase 1: Reveal (Memorize)
Tiles are revealed one at a time in sequence. Some tiles contain Skill Points marked with "SP" — memorize their positions. Duration: ~7 seconds.

### Phase 2: Hunt
The grid is hidden. Click tiles to find the SP you memorized. Limited number of attempts.

### Phase 3: Shuffle
After finding 2 or more SP, the grid shuffles to increase difficulty. Rows and columns slide in random directions.

### Phase 4: Results
Shows your performance and total earned SP.

### Phase 5: Allocate
Distribute your earned SP across three skills:
- **Split Aggro** — More splitting, but weaker children
- **Tether Resistance** — Break enemy tethers easier
- **Orb Power** — Higher maximum speed

Maximum of 4 points per skill.

## Configuration

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Grid Size | 6x6 (36 total tiles) |
| SP Tiles | 9 |
| Max Per Skill | 4 |

## Skill Point Conversion

```
multiplier = 1 + 0.5 * (1 - e^(-0.5 * points))
```

| Points | Multiplier | Bonus |
|--------|------------|-------|
| 0 | 1.00x | +0% |
| 1 | 1.11x | +11% |
| 2 | 1.18x | +18% |
| 3 | 1.22x | +22% |
| 4 | 1.24x | +24% |

## Determinism

The Matrix game is deterministic per player per round:
- Your personal seed is derived from: sha256(round_seed || tier_id || player || round_id)
- Same seed always produces same SP tile positions

---

# Early Exit (EE)

Early Exit is an optional mechanism that lets players exit early when they've earned enough.

## How It Works

1. Set a Target: When joining, specify a target earnings amount
2. Play Normally: Compete and earn bounty from kills
3. Auto-Exit: If your earnings hit the target, you automatically claim and exit
4. Orb Removed: Your orb is removed from the arena

## EE Presets

| Preset | Target | Strategy |
|--------|--------|----------|
| Safe | ~2 kills value | Minimize risk |
| Balanced | ~4 kills value | Moderate approach |
| Aggressive | ~6 kills value | High upside |
| YOLO | No EE | Maximum risk/reward |

## Edge Cases

- Kill resolves first; EE cannot "save" you from death
- Multiple EE same frame: Allowed, all qualifying players exit
- Final Survivor: EE is skipped; winner receives survival pool + remaining bounty
- EE applies per owner, not per orb

## Frame Processing Order

1. Elimination attribution → distribute kill bounty
2. Inheritance (if enabled)
3. EE checks → emit triggers
4. Prune EE'd orbs
5. Finalize if alive ≤ 1

---

# Skills & Strategy

PlayOrbs features a skill system that allows players to influence gameplay outcomes through strategic stat allocation.

## The Three Skills

### Tether Defense
Raises the speed threshold enemies need to break YOUR tethers. Defensive only.
```
effectiveBreakSpeed = baseBreakSpeed * tetherDefMul
```

### Power (Orb Speed)
Increases your orb's maximum velocity cap.
```
effectiveMaxSpeed = baseMaxSpeed * powerMul
```

### Split Aggression
Lowers your own split thresholds, making your orb more likely to split on high-impact collisions.
```
effectiveThreshold = baseThreshold / sqrt(splitAggroMul)
```
Tradeoff: Child orbs receive reduced Tether Defense (childTetherDef = parentTetherDef * 0.9, floored at 0.7x).

## Allocation Strategies

| Strategy | Split | Tether | Power | Playstyle |
|----------|-------|--------|-------|-----------|
| Balanced | 1-2 | 1-2 | 1-2 | Flexible |
| Aggressive | 0 | 3-4 | 1-2 | Focuses on eliminations |
| Swarm | 3-4 | 1 | 0 | Many orbs |
| Speed | 0 | 1-2 | 3-4 | Fast movement |

## Skill Interactions

| Your Build | Strong Against | Weak Against |
|------------|----------------|--------------|
| High Tether Def | Low-power attackers | High-power orbs |
| High Power | Evasive players | Swarm tactics |
| High Split Aggro | Single-orb players | AoE shockwaves |

---

# Points System

Points measure your performance and determine your share of season rewards.

## Points Constants

| Category | Value |
|----------|-------|
| 1st Place | +500 |
| 2nd Place | +350 |
| 3rd Place | +250 |
| Kill Bonus | +100 per kill |
| Participation | +50 |

## Calculation

```
points = 50 (participation floor)
if placement == 1: points += 500
if placement == 2: points += 350
if placement == 3: points += 250
points += kills × 100
points = points × tier_multiplier / 100
```

## Points Matrix

| Placement | 0 Kills | 1 Kill | 3 Kills | 5 Kills |
|-----------|---------|--------|---------|---------|
| 1st | 550 | 650 | 850 | 1,050 |
| 2nd | 400 | 500 | 700 | 900 |
| 3rd | 300 | 400 | 600 | 800 |
| 4th+ | 50 | 150 | 350 | 550 |

Values shown at 1.0x multiplier.

## Tier Multipliers

| Tier | Multiplier |
|------|------------|
| 0 | 1.0x |
| 1 | 1.5x |
| 2 | 2.0x |
| 3 | 3.0x |

## Points Accumulation

- **Season Points**: Reset each season, determine leaderboard ranking and pool share
- **Total Points**: Never reset, track lifetime achievement

---

# PORB Emissions

After Genesis, rounds can trigger PORB token emissions to early joiners and the season pool.

## Emission Distribution (250 PORB total)

| Recipient | Amount | Share |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| 1st Joiner | 100 PORB | 40% |
| 2nd Joiner | 60 PORB | 24% |
| 3rd Joiner | 40 PORB | 16% |
| Season Pool | 50 PORB | 20% |

## Emission Probability

- Probability increases by 5% per round since last emission
- At round 20: Guaranteed emission (100%)
- Average: ~1 emission per 10 rounds

## Epoch Decay

| Epoch | Years | Emission Rate |
|-------|-------|---------------|
| 0 | 0-2 | 250 PORB |
| 1 | 2-4 | 212 PORB |
| 2 | 4-6 | 180 PORB |
| 3 | 6-8 | 153 PORB |
| 4 | 8-10 | 130 PORB |

Each epoch is ~2 years. Emission rate decays by 15% per epoch.

## Supply Cap

- Total supply: 100,000,000 PORB
- Genesis mint: 500,000 PORB
- Post-genesis budget: 99,500,000 PORB
- When budget is exhausted, emissions stop permanently.

---

# Leaderboards

Players are ranked by season points. Higher points = higher leaderboard position.

## Climbing the Leaderboard

| Tier | Multiplier | Climb Speed |
|------|------------|-------------|
| 0 | 1.0x | Normal |
| 1 | 1.5x | 50% faster |
| 2 | 2.0x | 100% faster |
| 3 | 3.0x | 200% faster |

## Strategies

- **Volume**: Play many games at lower tiers for consistent small gains
- **High-Tier**: Fewer games but bigger points per game
- **Hybrid**: Mix tiers based on confidence

---

# Referral Program

## Rewards

| Reward Type | Amount |
|-------------|--------|
| SOL | 10% of their entry fee |
| Points | 20 points per join |

## Reward Flow

Referred Player Entry → 80% Prize Pool + 20% Protocol → 50% LP Vault + 50% Dev Split → 90% Dev + 10% Referrer

## Milestone Bonuses

| Milestone | Referrals | Bonus Points |
|-----------|-----------|--------------|
| Referrer Rookie | 1 | 500 |
| Referrer Pro | 50 | 5,000 |
| Referrer Legend | 250 | 10,000 |

## Rules

- Referrer is set permanently on first game
- Self-referral is blocked
- Rewards calculated per transaction from protocol fees

---

# Achievements

## Seasonal Achievements (reset each season)

| Achievement | Requirement |
|-------------|-------------|
| First Blood | Play 1 round |
| Getting Warm | Play 5 rounds |
| Committed | Play 25 rounds |
| Miner | Earn PORB emission |
| Champion | Win 1 round |
| Killing Spree | 3+ kills in one round |
| Hot Streak | 2 consecutive wins |

## Lifetime Achievements (one-time)

| Achievement | Requirement |
|-------------|-------------|
| Nickname Pioneer | First to set nickname |
| Referrer Rookie | Refer 1 player |
| Referrer Pro | Refer 50 players |
| Referrer Legend | Refer 250 players |

---

# PORB Token

## Token Specification

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Name | PORB |
| Symbol | PORB |
| Decimals | 9 |
| Smallest Unit | 1 atom = 10⁻⁹ PORB |
| Total Supply Cap | 100,000,000 PORB |
| Mint Authority | PDA-controlled |

## Token Utility

| Use Case | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Emissions Reward | First 3 joiners per round can earn PORB |
| Season Pool | Proportional rewards for leaderboard performance |
| Governance | Future voting on protocol parameters |
| Tournament Entry | Special events may require PORB |
| Cosmetics/NFTs | Unlock exclusive in-game items |

## Fair Launch Principles

| Allocation | Amount |
|------------|--------|
| Team/Advisors | 0% |
| Investors | 0% |
| Treasury | 0% |
| Gameplay | 100% |

## What Makes PORB Different

- **Liquidity-Backed**: Every PORB backed by real game fees in CLMM pool
- **Deflationary Pressure**: Buyback mechanism purchases PORB with protocol fees
- **Sustainable Emissions**: Epoch decay ensures 100+ year longevity
- **Verifiable Scarcity**: On-chain supply tracking, PDA-controlled mint

---

# Supply Mechanics

## Supply Overview

- Hard Cap: 100,000,000 PORB
- Genesis Mint: 500,000 PORB (0.5%) — Season 0 point conversions + initial CLMM liquidity
- Post-Genesis Emissions: 99,500,000 PORB (99.5%) — Probabilistic per-round emissions with epoch decay

## Supply Cap Enforcement

```rust
let cap_atoms = 100_000_000_000_000_000;
let remaining = cap_atoms - root.total_minted_orb;
let actual_mint = min(requested_mint, remaining);
root.total_minted_orb += actual_mint;
```

No minting possible beyond the cap. No private key exists—only the program can authorize mints.

## Long-Term Projections

| Epoch | Years | Rate | Annual | Cumulative |
|-------|-------|------|--------|------------|
| 0 | 0-2 | 250 PORB | ~6.5M | 6.5M |
| 1 | 2-4 | 212 PORB | ~5.5M | 12.0M |
| 2 | 4-6 | 180 PORB | ~4.7M | 16.7M |
| 10 | 20-22 | 49 PORB | ~1.3M | 37.0M |

Total projected emissions converge to ~43.8M PORB, well under the 99.5M budget.

---

# Genesis Bootstrap

Genesis is the one-time event that transitions from Season 0 to active token emissions.

## Genesis Trigger

When lp_accum_sol >= 25 SOL:

1. Mint 500,000 PORB
2. Create CLMM Pool: 250,000 PORB + 25 SOL on Raydium CLMM
   - Initial price: 0.0001 SOL/PORB
   - Range: -30% lower, ∞ upper
3. Lock Position NFT permanently
4. Enable Conversions: 50 points = 1 PORB, cap 250,000 PORB
5. Start Season 1 with emissions enabled

## Conversion Mechanics

```
base_ratio = 50 points per PORB
needed_ratio = max(50, ceil(total_points / 250,000))
```

If total season 0 points exceed what 250k PORB can cover at 50:1, the ratio increases.

## Genesis Parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Threshold | 25 SOL |
| Genesis PORB Cap | 500,000 PORB |
| Conversion Cap | 250,000 PORB |
| Base Rate | 50 points = 1 PORB |
| Initial Price | 0.0001 SOL/PORB |

---

# Epoch Decay

Emissions decrease over time through epoch-based decay.

## Epoch Definition

```
epoch_index = floor(total_rounds_since_genesis / epoch_rounds)
epoch_rounds = 525,600 (default)
At ~2 min/round: 1 epoch ≈ 2 years
```

## Decay Formula

```
E(n) = E₀ × r^n
E₀ = base emission (250 PORB)
r = retention rate (0.85)
n = epoch_index
```

## Emission Schedule

| Epoch | Years | Per Emission | ~Annual |
|-------|-------|--------------|---------|
| 0 | 0-2 | 250 PORB | 6.5M |
| 1 | 2-4 | 212 PORB | 5.5M |
| 2 | 4-6 | 180 PORB | 4.7M |
| 5 | 10-12 | 111 PORB | 2.9M |
| 10 | 20-22 | 49 PORB | 1.3M |
| 20 | 40-42 | 10 PORB | 0.3M |

## Mathematical Proof of Convergence

```
Total = E₀ × N × (1 / (1 - r))
     = 250 × 26,280 × 6.67
     ≈ 43.8M PORB
```

Well under the 99.5M budget.

## Decay Constants

| Constant | Value |
|----------|-------|
| EPOCH_ROUNDS_DEFAULT | 525,600 |
| DECAY_BPS_DEFAULT | 8,500 (85% retention) |
| EMIT_BASE_DEFAULT | 250 PORB |

---

# Liquidity Management

The protocol uses Raydium's Concentrated Liquidity Market Maker (CLMM).

## CLMM Integration

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Pool Type | Raydium CLMM |
| Token Pair | PORB / WSOL |
| Initial Price | 0.0001 SOL/PORB |
| Price Range | -30% to +∞ |
| Slippage Tolerance | 500 bps (5%) |

## Buyback Mechanism

When LP vault accumulates sufficient SOL:
1. Swap 50% of vault: WSOL → PORB via CLMM
2. Remaining 50% WSOL + bought PORB → increase liquidity
3. Repeat as fees accumulate

## Position Locking

The CLMM position NFT is permanently locked:
- Cannot be withdrawn
- Guarantees permanent liquidity
- Prevents rug pulls

## Self-Reinforcing Loop

More players → more fees → more buybacks → more liquidity → better UX → more players

---

# Economic Sustainability

The PlayOrbs economy is designed as a self-sustaining loop where gameplay generates real value.

## Key Economic Properties

| Property | Mechanism |
|----------|-----------|
| Non-Inflationary | Hard cap at 100M PORB |
| Liquidity-Backed | Every PORB backed by real game fees |
| Deflationary Pressure | Buybacks add PORB to locked liquidity |
| Sustainable Emissions | Epoch decay prevents rapid depletion |
| Real Yield | SOL prizes from actual player entry fees |

## Why This Works

1. **Real Value In**: Players pay SOL to play (external market value)
2. **Real Value Out**: Winners receive SOL prizes (not dependent on token price)
3. **Token Value Creation**: Fees fund buybacks, buybacks create demand, locked liquidity supports price floor
4. **Long-Term Viability**: Epoch decay extends emissions 100+ years

## Economic Phases

- **Early (Years 0-2)**: High emissions attract players, community builds, liquidity deepens
- **Growth (Years 2-6)**: Emissions decay, buybacks become significant, price stability improves
- **Maturity (Years 6+)**: Reduced emissions, buyback dominates, strong liquidity
- **Steady State**: Fixed supply, value from utility only, self-sustaining through fees

---

# Architecture Overview

PlayOrbs is a dual-chain application powered by Solana and the Internet Computer (ICP).

## Program Identification

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Program ID | CZGSRyEqc9RkCsGbknF92FQQJqPF7SzQDH7avmfRUaqd |
| Framework | Anchor 0.32.1 |
| Runtime | Solana BPF |
| Token Standard | SPL Token (9 decimals) |

## Core Components

### On-Chain Layer
- **RootAccount**: Global configuration and state
- **RoundPages**: Paged storage for round data
- **PlayerStats**: Per-season player statistics
- **SeasonSnapshots**: Frozen season data for claims

### ICP — Randomness Layer
- Threshold Randomness: Seeds generated by distributed subnet nodes
- Merkle Proofs: Cryptographic binding of seeds to specific rounds
- ECDSA Signatures: Non-repudiable proof of authenticity
- Unpredictable: No one can predict or influence outcomes

### Raydium — Liquidity Layer
- Concentrated liquidity for PORB/WSOL
- Automated buyback mechanism
- Locked position for permanent liquidity

## Security Model

| Layer | Mechanism |
|-------|-----------|
| Access Control | Authority-based with has_one constraints |
| Membership | PDA-based proofs (RoundPlayer) |
| Idempotency | Flag-based tracking |
| Randomness | ICP threshold cryptography |
| Funds | PDA-controlled vaults |

---

# Account Structures

## Account Summary

| Account | PDA Seeds | Size | Purpose |
|---------|-----------|------|---------|
| RootAccount | ["root"] | ~560 bytes | Global configuration & state |
| RoundPage | ["round_page", tier_id, page_index] | ~23.5 KB | Paged round storage |
| RoundPlayer | ["rp", tier_id, round_id, player] | 86 bytes | Membership proof |
| PlayerStats | ["ps", season_id, player] | 129 bytes | Per-season statistics |
| Vault | ["vault", tier_id, round_id] | ~165 bytes | Prize pool escrow |
| SeasonSnapshot | ["season_snapshot", season_id] | 59 bytes | Frozen season data |

## RootAccount

```rust
struct RootAccount {
    authority: Pubkey,
    dev_wallet: Pubkey,
    orb_mint: Pubkey,
    season_id: u64,
    season_rounds_total: u64,
    season_rounds_played: u64,
    current_season_total_points: u128,
    season_pool_orb: u128,
    genesis_done: bool,
    conversion_open: bool,
    lp_accum_sol: u64,
    genesis_threshold_lamports: u64,
    genesis_orb_cap: u64,
    orbs_minted_from_conversion: u64,
    points_per_orb_effective: u64,
    total_minted_orb: u128,
    emission_budget_cap: u128,
    total_rounds_since_genesis: u64,
    last_emission_round: u64,
    epoch_index: u32,
    epoch_rounds: u64,
    decay_bps: u16,
    hazard_cap_rounds: u64,
    emit_base: u128,
    tier_configs: Vec<TierConfig>,
    tier_states: Vec<TierState>,
    dev_fee_bps: u16,
}
```

## RoundPlayer

```rust
struct RoundPlayer {
    tier_id: u8,
    round_id: u64,
    player: Pubkey,
    payout_processed: bool,
    stats_applied: bool,
    account_index: u16,
    referrer: Option<Pubkey>,
}
```

## PlayerStats

```rust
struct PlayerStats {
    season_id: u64,
    player: Pubkey,
    season_points: u64,
    total_points: u64,
    rounds_played: u32,
    wins: u32,
    kills: u32,
    sol_earned_lamports: u64,
    orb_earned_atoms: u64,
    season_pool_claimed: bool,
}
```

## Storage Optimization

- Rounds stored in pages: Page Index = floor(round_id / 120), Slot Index = round_id % 120
- PDA-based membership reduces transaction size and enables efficient RPC filtering

---

# Instruction Reference

The PlayOrbs program exposes 18 instructions.

## Player Instructions

- **join_round**: Player enters round by paying entry fee
- **convert_points**: Convert Season 0 points to PORB (post-genesis)
- **claim_season_pool**: Claim proportional share of season pool
- **init_referral**: Set referrer (one-time)
- **claim_referral_rewards**: Claim accumulated referral SOL rewards
- **set_nickname**: Set unique player nickname

## Authority Instructions

- **initialize_root**: One-time program setup
- **settle_round**: Set seed, trigger emissions
- **round_payout**: Distribute SOL prizes
- **update_player_stats**: Award points
- **close_round_player**: Reclaim rent
- **check_and_run_genesis**: Trigger genesis

## Liquidity Instructions

- **open_position**: Create initial CLMM position
- **increase_liquidity**: Add to CLMM
- **lock_position**: Lock position NFT permanently
- **buyback**: WSOL → PORB swap via CLMM

---

# Security Model

## Access Control Matrix

| Operation | Signer Required |
|-----------|-----------------|
| join_round | Player |
| settle_round | Authority |
| round_payout | Authority |
| update_player_stats | Authority |
| convert_points | Player |
| claim_season_pool | Player |
| manage_liquidity | Authority |

## Security Mechanisms

- **PDA-Based Membership**: Membership proven via PDAs, no array size limits
- **Idempotency Tracking**: Flags prevent double-processing (payout_processed, stats_applied, did_emit, season_pool_claimed)
- **Vault Security**: PDA-controlled vaults, no private key exists
- **Arithmetic Safety**: Saturating operations, no floating-point
- **Supply Cap Enforcement**: Hard cap on-chain, min(requested, remaining)

## Attack Vectors & Mitigations

| Attack | Mitigation |
|--------|------------|
| Double-spend payout | payout_processed flag |
| Double-claim points | stats_applied flag |
| Emission manipulation | Deterministic SHA-256 |
| Supply inflation | Hard cap + tracking |
| Vault drain | PDA-only access |
| Front-running | ICP seed generation |
| Rug pull | Locked LP position |

---

# Settlement Flow

## Pipeline

1. Game Ends (off-chain)
2. Generate Seed (ICP Canister)
3. Determine Winners (off-chain logic)
4. Call settleRound (seed + signature)
5. Call roundPayout (distribute SOL)
6. Call updatePlayerStats (award points)
7. Call closeRoundPlayers (reclaim rent)

---

# Randomness Overview

Fair randomness is essential for trustless gaming. PlayOrbs uses ICP threshold cryptography.

## Three Key Properties

1. **Cryptographic Unpredictability**: Seeds from ICP's distributed randomness (requires supermajority collusion to predict)
2. **Public Verifiability**: Every seed has a Merkle proof and ECDSA signature
3. **Temporal Binding**: Seeds cryptographically bound to specific rounds

## Trust Model

| Assumption | Description |
|------------|-------------|
| ICP Network Security | Supermajority of subnet nodes must be honest |
| Cryptographic Hardness | SHA-256 and ECDSA remain secure |
| Canister Integrity | Deployed code matches audited source |

Administrators are NOT trusted for seed generation.

## Key Parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Chunk Size | 50 seeds |
| Seed Size | 32 bytes |
| Hash Function | SHA-256 |
| Signature Curve | secp256k1 |

---

# ICP Integration

The ICP Orbs canister provides cryptographically verifiable random seeds.

## The Chunk Model

Seeds are produced in batches of 50:
- Chunk 0: Rounds 0-49
- Chunk 1: Rounds 50-99
- chunk_id = round_id / 50

## Canister Functions

- **generate_and_store_chunk**: Generates 50 seeds, builds Merkle tree, signs root
- **reveal_seed_for_round**: Returns seed with full verification proof
- **chunk_exists**: Verifies if a chunk has been generated

## Entropy Source

ICP's raw_rand() function generates randomness through threshold BLS signatures across subnet nodes. A supermajority would need to collude to predict the output.

---

# Seed Generation

Seeds are derived from ICP's threshold randomness and committed via Merkle trees.

## Derivation Process

1. Obtain master seed from raw_rand() (32 bytes)
2. Derive 50 individual seeds: seed[i] = SHA256(master_seed || i.to_be_bytes())
3. Immediately discard master seed

## Merkle Tree Construction

### Leaf Construction
```
leaf_hash = SHA256("orbs-leaf" || season_id || tier_id || round_id || seed)
```

The round_id prevents proof reuse across rounds.

### Signed Message Format
```
"OrbsChunkRoot\nSeason:{season_id}\nTier:{tier_id}\nChunk:{chunk_id}\nRoot:{root_hex}"
```

Signed with ICP's threshold ECDSA (secp256k1).

---

# Sequential Revelation

Seeds are revealed in strict sequence to prevent gaming.

## Enforced Ordering

1. Each (season, tier) pair maintains a last_settled_round counter
2. Seed requests only honored for last_settled_round + 1
3. After successful revelation, counter increments
4. Requests for already-revealed rounds return cached proofs
5. Requests for future rounds are rejected

## Preventing Gaming

| Attack | Mitigation |
|--------|------------|
| Pre-fetch future seeds | Rejected—counter must match |
| Selective round avoidance | Seeds unknown until settlement |
| Replay old proofs | Round-ID binding invalidates them |

---

# Verification

Every seed proof undergoes a four-step verification process on-chain.

## Step 1: Chunk ID Validation
Ensures seed comes from correct chunk: expected_chunk_id = round_id / 50

## Step 2: ECDSA Signature Verification
Recovers public key from signature, compares to known ICP canister key.

## Step 3: Leaf Hash Computation
```
leaf_hash = SHA256("orbs-leaf" || season_id || tier_id || round_id || seed)
```
Binds seed to THIS specific round.

## Step 4: Merkle Proof Verification
Recomputes path from leaf to root, verifies computed_root == merkle_root.

## Security Properties

| Property | Mechanism | Attack Prevented |
|----------|-----------|------------------|
| Authenticity | ECDSA signature | Fake seed injection |
| Integrity | Merkle proof | Seed tampering |
| Uniqueness | Round-ID binding | Cross-round reuse |
| Pre-commitment | ICP canister storage | Authority manipulation |

---

# Engine Overview

The Orbs Battle Game runs on a fully deterministic physics engine at 120 Hz.

## Core Principles

| Principle | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Fixed Timestep | 120 FPS physics simulation |
| Determinism | Identical outcomes from identical seeds |
| Modularity | Configurable parameters |
| Blockchain-Ready | Cryptographic hashing of configs and states |

## Current Configuration

| Category | Settings |
|----------|----------|
| Arena | Circle, 190px radius |
| Physics | Slightly super-elastic (1.08 wall, 1.008 orb-orb) |
| Speed | 4.0-9.5 normal, up to 25.0 endgame |
| Spawning | Ring-based, stationary start |
| Gravity | Minimal base, oscillates below 3 orbs |
| Tethers | 200-frame immunity, 7.5 break speed minimum |
| Splits | Disabled |
| Sudden Death | At 5 seconds, 16x gravity, spiral center |
| Shockwaves | Enabled, triggered by impacts and splits |

---

# Collision Physics

Orbs collide elastically, transferring momentum and potentially triggering eliminations.

## Collision Detection

Two orbs collide when their centers are closer than the sum of their radii.

## Configuration

| Parameter | Current Value |
|-----------|---------------|
| orbRestitution | 1.004 |

Current config adds very slight energy, creating gradually faster gameplay.

## Collision Events

- **Tether Breaking**: If colliding orb is fast enough and hits a tether
- **Shockwaves**: High-impact collisions emit shockwaves that cut nearby tethers
- **Eliminations**: When all of a player's tethers are broken

## Orb Properties

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| radius | 12px |
| baseSpeed | 2.0 |
| colors | 6 |

---

# Gravity System

Central gravity pulls orbs toward the arena center, with optional oscillation.

## Current Configuration

- Base Gravity: 0.000001 (very subtle)
- Amplitude: 40% of base
- Period: 40 frames (~0.33 seconds)
- Activates Below: 3 orbs

## Oscillation Behavior

When oscillation activates: gravity pulses between 0.6x and 1.4x of base at 40-frame intervals.

---

# Tether System

Tethers are protective lines connecting orbs to boundary anchor points. Breaking all tethers eliminates a player.

## Current Configuration

- Hit Damping: 0.125 (12.5% speed reduction)
- Spring: Disabled (K=0)
- Break Speed Minimum: 7.5
- Immunity: 200 frames (~1.67 seconds)

## Breaking Conditions

1. Immunity has expired
2. Colliding orb speed ≥ breakSpeedMin
3. Orb intersects the tether line

## Elimination

All tethers broken → Player eliminated → Kill credited → Orb removed

---

# Boundary System

## Current Configuration

- Shape: Circle
- Radius: 190px
- Restitution: 1.02 (slightly accelerating)
- Tangent Impulse: 0.01 (minimal sideways kick)
- Min Speed: 2.0
- Max Speed: 8.0 (normal), 20.0 (endgame)
- Endgame Ramp: 400 frames (~3.3 seconds)
- Direction: Random per bounce (CW/CCW)

---

# Sudden Death

Sudden death activates when matches run long, dramatically increasing intensity.

## Current Configuration

- Trigger: 600 frames (~5 seconds when 2 orbs remain)
- Gravity: 2x multiplier
- Center Shift: 0.1 radius, 150-frame period
- Tangent Impulse: 5x multiplier
- Restitution: 1.2x multiplier

## Effects

- Boundary bounces become more chaotic
- Collisions are more energetic
- Gravity center spirals, creating a whirlpool
- Orb splits allowed in endgame

---

# Split Mechanic

When orbs collide with enough force, they can split into two smaller orbs.

## Current Configuration

- Enabled below 2 orbs (endgame only)
- VN Threshold: 5.5
- KE Threshold: 25.0
- Radius Scale: 1.0 (same size)
- Child Speed: 0.5x parent speed
- Max Generations: 2
- Cooldown: 180 frames
- Max Orbs: 24

## Ownership Tracking

Split children inherit the parent's owner. Game tracks unique players, not orb count:
- Game ends when countUniqueOwners(orbs) <= 1

---

# Parameter Reference

## Boundary

| Parameter | Default |
|-----------|---------|
| shape | circle |
| radius | 190 |
| restitution | 1.08 |
| tangentImpulse | 0.02 |
| minSpeed | 4.0 |
| maxSpeed | 9.5 |
| twoOrbsMaxSpeed | 25.0 |
| twoOrbsRampFrames | 300 |

## Orb

| Parameter | Default |
|-----------|---------|
| radius | 12 |
| baseSpeed | 4.0 |
| orbRestitution | 1.008 |

## Gravity

| Parameter | Default |
|-----------|---------|
| base | 0.000001 |
| ampFrac | 0.4 |
| periodFrames | 40 |
| oscillateBelowOrbs | 3 |

## Tether

| Parameter | Default |
|-----------|---------|
| hitDamping | 0.125 |
| breakSpeedMin | 7.5 |
| immunityFrames | 200 |

## Shockwave

| Parameter | Default |
|-----------|---------|
| enabled | true |
| lifeFrames | 40 |
| maxRadius | 100 |
| ringThickness | 5 |
| impactThreshold | 8.0 |

## Split

| Parameter | Default |
|-----------|---------|
| enabled | false |
| vnThreshold | 10 |
| keThreshold | 100 |
| radiusScale | 0.7 |
| childSpeedMul | 0.8 |
| maxGenerations | 2 |

## Sudden Death

| Parameter | Default |
|-----------|---------|
| enabled | true |
| afterFrames | 600 |
| durationFrames | 360 |
| gravityMultiplier | 16 |
| centerShiftRadius | 100 |
| orbAttraction | 0.5 |

## Spawn

| Parameter | Default |
|-----------|---------|
| mode | rings |
| pad | 58 |
| startInset | 104 |
| ringGap | 30 |
| velocity | none |

---

# Fee Distribution

Entry fees follow a transparent 80/20 split.

## Fee Flow

| Destination | Percentage |
|-------------|------------|
| Prize Vault | 80% |
| Protocol Fees | 20% |

### Protocol Fee Breakdown

| Component | Share |
|-----------|-------|
| LP Vault | 50% |
| Dev Split | 50% |
| Referral | 10% of Dev |

## Example (1 SOL entry)

- Prize Pool: 0.800 SOL
- LP Vault: 0.020 SOL
- Dev Wallet: 0.018 SOL
- Referrer: 0.002 SOL (if applicable)

## Fee Invariants

1. No protocol retention: 100% of vault goes to players
2. Integer arithmetic: All calculations in lamports
3. Deterministic: Same inputs always produce same outputs
4. Transparent: All fees visible on-chain

---

# Reward Distribution

The prize pool (80% of entries) splits into bounty (70%) and survival (30%) pools.

## Example Pool (10 players at 1 SOL each)

- Player Pool: 8.00 SOL (80%)
- Bounty Pool: 5.60 SOL (70% of player pool)
- Survival Pool: 2.40 SOL (30% of player pool)

## Bounty Distribution

Distributed per kill using a log-weighted schedule. Early kills earn more than late kills.

## Survival Distribution

Goes to the last player standing.

## Final Winner Reward

Winner receives survival pool + any remaining bounty.

---

# Kill Rewards

Kill rewards follow a log-weighted schedule where early kills pay more.

## Weight Function

w(A) = 1 + log₂(A)

| Alive Before | w(A) |
|--------------|------|
| 2 | 2.00 |
| 5 | 3.32 |
| 10 | 4.32 |
| 32 | 6.00 |
| 64 | 7.00 |

## Properties

- Exact sum equals bounty pool (rounding remainder to earliest kills)
- Schedule precomputed at round start, never rescaled
- Self-elimination: no kill credit, bounty goes to winner

## Scaling with Player Count

| Players | First Kill | Last Kill | Ratio |
|---------|------------|-----------|-------|
| 5 | ~1.25x avg | ~0.75x avg | 1.66x |
| 10 | ~1.40x avg | ~0.65x avg | 2.16x |
| 50 | ~1.70x avg | ~0.45x avg | 3.78x |

---

# Bounty Inheritance

When Player A kills Player B, B's uncashed bounty transfers to A.

## Kill Chain Effect

Eliminating high-earners is valuable—their accumulated bounty transfers to the killer.

## Inheritance + Early Exit

Inheritance can instantly trigger EE if it pushes earnings over the target.

## Invariants

1. No minting: Transfers only move existing credits
2. Sum preservation: Total bounty earned never exceeds pool
3. Deterministic: Same game state produces same inheritance

---

# Tie Breaking

When all remaining players are eliminated simultaneously, tie-break rules determine the winner.

## Priority

| Priority | Criterion | Higher Wins |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| 1 | framesAlive | Yes |
| 2 | kills | Yes |
| 3 | rosterIndex | No (lower wins) |

Roster index (join order) guarantees a unique winner.

---

# Glossary

## Game Terms
- **Orb**: Player's in-game representation
- **Tether**: Protective line connecting orb to boundary anchor
- **Round**: Single game session from open to settlement
- **Tier**: Competition level with specific entry fee and multiplier
- **Season**: Time-bounded competition period
- **Arena**: Circular boundary (190px radius default)

## Economic Terms
- **Bounty Pool**: 70% of prize pool, distributed via kills
- **Survival Pool**: 30% of prize pool, awarded to last standing
- **Kill Reward**: SOL payment for eliminations (log-weighted)
- **Early Exit (EE)**: Claim accumulated rewards early
- **Bounty Inheritance**: Victim's uncashed bounty transfers to killer
- **Lamports**: Smallest unit of SOL (1 SOL = 1,000,000,000 lamports)

## Token Terms
- **PORB**: Native token, earned through gameplay
- **Emission**: Minting new PORB as rewards
- **Epoch**: Period defining emission decay cycles
- **Genesis Pool**: Initial PORB/SOL liquidity pool
- **CLMM**: Concentrated Liquidity Market Maker (Raydium)

## Technical Terms
- **PDA**: Program Derived Address
- **Anchor**: Solana framework for smart contracts
- **Settlement**: Finalizing round with seed verification
- **Seed**: Cryptographic randomness for deterministic outcomes
- **Merkle Proof**: Proof verifying seed inclusion in committed tree
- **ICP**: Internet Computer Protocol
- **Canister**: Smart contract on ICP

## Physics Terms
- **Restitution**: Energy multiplier during collisions
- **Gravity**: Central force pulling orbs to center
- **Shockwave**: Expanding ring from collisions that cuts tethers
- **Sudden Death**: Endgame phase with increased gravity
- **Immunity Frames**: Protection period for new tethers

## Abbreviations
- SOL: Solana token
- PORB: PlayOrbs token
- EE: Early Exit
- LP: Liquidity Provider
- PDA: Program Derived Address
- ICP: Internet Computer Protocol
- CLMM: Concentrated Liquidity Market Maker
- BPS: Basis Points (1/100 of 1%)

---

# Constants Reference

## Token Constants

| Constant | Value |
|----------|-------|
| TOKEN_DECIMALS | 9 |
| TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP | 100,000,000 |
| INITIAL_EMISSION | 10 PORB |
| DECAY_RATE | 0.85 |
| EPOCH_LENGTH | 10 |

## Fee Constants

| Constant | Value |
|----------|-------|
| VAULT_SHARE | 80% |
| PROTOCOL_SHARE | 20% |
| LP_SHARE | 50% |
| REFERRAL_SHARE | 10% |
| DEV_FEE_BPS | 2000 |

## Pool Constants

| Constant | Value |
|----------|-------|
| BOUNTY_SHARE | 70% |
| SURVIVAL_SHARE | 30% |

## Scoring Constants

| Constant | Value |
|----------|-------|
| FIRST_PLACE_PTS | 500 |
| SECOND_PLACE_PTS | 350 |
| THIRD_PLACE_PTS | 250 |
| KILL_PTS | 100 |
| PARTICIPATION_FLOOR_PTS | 50 |

## Configuration Formulas

- Kill Weight: w(A) = 1 + log₂(A)
- Epoch Emission: E(n) = E₀ × (0.85)^n
- Gravity Oscillation: g(t) = base × (1 + ampFrac × sin(2π × t / period))
- Emission Probability: P(emit) = 1 - e^(-λ × Σorbits)

---

# Mathematical Proofs

## Proof 1: Kill Reward Exact Sum
The sum of all kill rewards equals the bounty pool. Implementation distributes rounding remainder to early kills.

## Proof 2: Emission Decay Convergence
Total emissions converge to ~43.8M PORB (well under 99.5M budget). T(∞) = e × E₀ × (1 / (1 - d)).

## Proof 3: Fee Distribution Conservation
All entry fees are distributed without loss or gain through integer arithmetic.

## Proof 4: Bounty Inheritance Invariant
Inheritance never creates new value. Net change in sum is always zero.

## Proof 5: Hazard Rate Probability
The emission probability function produces values in [0, 1] and is monotonically increasing.

## Proof 6: Tie-Break Uniqueness
The tie-break rule chain always produces exactly one winner (guaranteed by unique roster index).

## Proof 7: Log-Weight Monotonicity
Kill rewards decrease as fewer players remain (log is monotonic).

---

# SDK: Join Round

The @playorbs/sdk provides a simple API to join game rounds programmatically.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @playorbs/sdk
# Peer dependencies:
npm install @coral-xyz/anchor @solana/web3.js bn.js
```

## Quick Start

```typescript
import { OrbsGameSDK } from "@playorbs/sdk";
import { Connection, Keypair } from "@solana/web3.js";
import { AnchorProvider, Wallet } from "@coral-xyz/anchor";

const connection = new Connection("https://api.mainnet-beta.solana.com");
const wallet = new Wallet(Keypair.generate());
const provider = new AnchorProvider(connection, wallet, { commitment: "confirmed" });
const sdk = new OrbsGameSDK({ provider });
```

## API Reference

### joinRound(options)

```typescript
interface JoinRoundOptions {
  roundId: number;
  tierId: number;
  player: Keypair | WalletSigner;
  tpPreset?: number;    // 0=Disabled, 1=Safe, 2=Balanced, 3=Fierce, 4=YOLO
  referrer?: PublicKey;
}
// Returns: { signature: string }
```

### getNextRoundId(tierId: number)

Get the next available round ID for a tier.

## Error Handling

| Error | Cause |
|-------|-------|
| RoundNotOpen | Round is full or already settled |
| AlreadyJoined | Player already in this round |
| InsufficientFunds | Not enough SOL for entry fee |

---

# Legal: Terms and Conditions

Updated February 2026. Full terms available at https://docs.playorbs.com/legal/terms

Key points:
- Players must be 18+ and not in excluded jurisdictions (US, UK, Germany, Netherlands, sanctioned countries)
- Non-custodial wallet-based access
- Entry fees are non-refundable once collected by Smart Contract
- No guarantee of any return on entry fees
- PORB is not a security or investment product
- PORB has no guaranteed value, price floor, or liquidity
- Points have no monetary value
- Service provided "as is" without warranties
- Governed by Singapore law
- Binding arbitration via Singapore International Arbitration Centre

---

# Legal: Privacy Policy

Updated February 2026. Full policy available at https://docs.playorbs.com/legal/privacy_policy

Key points:
- Non-custodial, wallet-based model (no accounts, emails, or passwords required)
- Data processed: wallet/blockchain data, network/security data, support communications, nicknames
- Purposes: security, fraud prevention, jurisdictional compliance, dispute handling, platform operations
- No profiling, behavioral tracking, or targeted advertising
- Lawful bases: legitimate interests and legal obligations
- Data retention: limited and transient for network data; blockchain data is public
- Data not sold
- Contact: privacy@playorbs.com
